parsed.summary: The commit renames the npm scope and adds an interactive menu to the setup command. The scope rename from `@anthropic` to `@anthropics` is a breaking change for existing installations — users who installed under the old scope will have a broken plugin reference with no migration path documented. The interactive menu in a markdown-based command file (`commands/setup.md`) relies on prompt_toolkit but has no error handling for missing dependencies or non-interactive environments. The release_card.md and CHANGELOG entries do not clearly communicate the breaking nature of the scope rename.

findings.title: npm scope rename is a breaking change with no migration guidance
findings.body: The package.json shows a scope rename from the old scope to a new one. Existing users who have the plugin installed under the old scope will find their installation broken after this publishes. There is no migration script, no deprecation notice on the old package, and the CHANGELOG does not flag this as breaking. The release_card.md mentions the rename but buries it rather than leading with it as a breaking action item.
findings.recommendation: Either publish under both scopes (old as a redirect/deprecated), or add a clear migration section to CHANGELOG.md and release_card.md as a BREAKING CHANGE with concrete uninstall/reinstall steps. Consider a preinstall script that detects the old scope and warns.

findings.title: Interactive menu in setup command has no fallback for non-interactive or dependency-missing environments
findings.body: The commands/setup.md change adds an interactive menu with prompt_toolkit-based selection. If prompt_toolkit is not installed, or if the environment is non-interactive (piped input, CI, headless), the command will fail with an unhandled exception rather than a graceful degradation message. No try/except or fallback path is visible in the command definition.
findings.recommendation: Add a guard that catches ImportError for prompt_toolkit and falls back to a simple text-based prompt or aborts with a clear message. Also handle EOFError for non-interactive shells.

findings.title: CHANGELOG does not use conventional semver breaking-change annotation
findings.body: The CHANGELOG entry for what appears to be v0.4.7 describes the scope rename and interactive menu but does not mark the scope rename as a BREAKING CHANGE in semver convention. Given this is a version bump that includes a rename affecting installed users, the lack of a clear breaking annotation increases the risk of users upgrading without realizing they need to take action.
findings.recommendation: Add a 'BREAKING CHANGE' prefix or section to the CHANGELOG entry for the scope rename, and ensure the version number reflects the breaking nature if following semver.

next_steps: Verify the exact old and new npm scope names in package.json and confirm whether the old package name has been deprecated on npm
Test the setup command in a non-interactive environment (piped stdin) to confirm failure mode
Add migration instructions for existing users to CHANGELOG.md and the release card before publishing
Confirm the plugin.json and marketplace.json references are consistent with the new scope name
